other_observers: none; No. Reported from 6/07/2010 via Cobirds. Seen later on 6/08/2010 by Cole Wild.
specific_location: south side of picnic pavilion at Crow Valley Campground (Pawnee National Grassland)
description: passserine bird size and shape of a warbler; bright yellow below with black streaks on sides of breast; olive upperparts with dull wingbars; dull reddish-colored streaks on back; face yelllow with black markings characteristic of Prairie Warbler.Behaviors: flitting in shrubs and small treesCall: song: rising series of buzzy notesPlumage: alternate
